comments.text: Claimed proof (preprint): Parikshit Chalise, Antwan Clark, and Edinah K. Gnang posted “A Proof of the Tree Packing Conjecture” (arXiv:2410.13840; v2 dated 23 Oct 2024), which claims to prove Gyárfás’ Tree Packing Conjecture for all $n$. https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.13840 High-level outline (as stated by the authors): The paper introduces a reformulation of tree packing into $K_n$ via “augmented functional trees” and reduces the existence of an edge-disjoint packing of $(T_2,\dots,T_n)$ into $K_n$ to a “complete labeling” problem. They define a “tree packing polynomial certificate” $P_g(X,y)=V(X)E_g(X,y)$ and (Proposition 3.4) relate existence of a complete labeling to a non-vanishing condition for a canonical representative of $P_g$. A key step is the “Composition Lemma” (Lemma 3.10), asserting that the relevant non-vanishing property is preserved under a local composition operation; iterating this is used to reduce general instances to a star-based instance, yielding Theorem 1.9 and the main claimed result. Status note: This is a preprint; I have not found a refereed journal version or a widely circulated independent verification. There is community discussion about related “composition lemma” approaches here: https://mathoverflow.net/questions/482509/claimed-proofs-of-graph-labelling-conjectures Suggestion for this page: It may be useful to add arXiv:2410.13840 as a reference (possibly marked “claimed proof / pending verification”), and update the open/solved status if/when a vetted confirmation appears.
I've known people who tried to verify this paper. they did not have faith in the present arguments.

Alright, I tried applying GPT-based "standard check" (because why not?). The process claims to identify 2 issues that are at least major gaps, as well as a large number of other issues that are potentially less serious. As usual, claims are not guaranteed.
